correctness.description: Does DELETE /todos/{id} work, and does the rest of the API still pass its tests?
signals: DELETE removes the todo and it no longer appears in GET /todos
Existing GET and POST endpoints are unaffected

edge_case_handling.description: Does deleting a non-existent todo fail gracefully?
signals: DELETE on an unknown id returns 404, not 500 or a silent 200
Deleting the same id twice doesn't crash on the second attempt

code_readability.description: Does the new endpoint match the style and conventions of the existing endpoints?
signals: Consistent status codes (204 for delete, matching the existing 201 for create)
Reuses the existing lookup/404 pattern instead of duplicating logic differently

pattern_recognition.description: Does the user understand REST conventions for delete semantics?
signals: Recognizes DELETE should return 404 (not 500) for unknown resources, mirroring GET
Understands why 204 No Content is the conventional response for a successful delete

what_senior_would_catch.description: One specific thing a real engineer would flag in review.
signals: A senior engineer would ask: what happens if two clients try to delete the same todo at the same time?
